text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. When a developer writes new code, a colleague reviews it before it is added to the main project. This process helps catch bugs early, before they become more expensive and difficult to fix. Additionally, code reviews encourage knowledge sharing within the team. Junior developers can learn from more experienced team members, while senior developers get fresh perspectives. Over time, this strengthens the entire team's quality and consistency. A team that regularly reviews code builds trust and produces more reliable software.
